home.heroSubtitle: CrawSecure analyzes ClawHub skills for dangerous patterns before you install them. Analysis runs entirely on your machine — your files never leave.
home.howSubtitle: Three steps, zero uploads, instant results.
home.step1Title: Drop files or run the CLI
home.step1Desc: Drag project files into the browser scanner, or run `npx crawsecure .` from any directory.
home.step2Desc: The engine reads file contents in memory and applies 13 security rules. Nothing leaves your device.
home.step3Desc: See which rules fired, severity levels, and an overall risk score — instantly.
home.featSubtitle: Every design decision prioritises your privacy and security.
home.feat1Desc: All scans run in your browser or CLI. Your code is never uploaded, stored, or transmitted.
home.feat2Desc: CrawSecure never executes code. It reads, inspects, and reports — nothing more.
home.feat3Desc: Every scan produces a scored report: SAFE, MEDIUM, or HIGH — with per-rule breakdowns.
home.feat4Desc: Generate a crawsecure.json with the CLI and load it into the web UI for visualization.
home.feat5Desc: Detects dangerous commands, process spawning, file deletion, network access, and sensitive file references.
home.feat6Desc: The scan rules are public. Audit exactly what we look for — no black boxes.
home.rulesSubtitle: 13 stable rule IDs — all public, all auditable.
home.privTitle: Your code never touches our servers. Ever.
home.privBody: Open DevTools → Network tab while running a scan. You will see zero outbound requests during analysis. If you choose to save a scan, you'll see exactly what is sent — a handful of numbers, nothing more.
home.faq1a: Never. All analysis runs in your browser or CLI on your machine. File contents are processed in memory and discarded immediately. The server only ever receives aggregated numbers — score, rule IDs, file count.
home.faq2q: What does the server actually store if I save a scan?
home.faq2a: Only: overall risk score, the list of rule IDs that fired, total file count, and severity counts. No file names, no file paths, no code snippets. You can verify this by reading the open-source POST /api/scans handler.
home.faq3q: Can I use CrawSecure in CI/CD?
home.faq3a: Yes. Run `npx crawsecure .` in any pipeline step. The CLI exits with code 0 for SAFE, code 2 for HIGH risk — which most CI systems treat as a failure.
home.faq4q: What file types does CrawSecure scan?
home.faq4a: JavaScript (.js, .mjs, .cjs), TypeScript (.ts, .tsx), Shell scripts (.sh, .bash), JSON config files, and YAML. Binary files and node_modules are automatically excluded.
home.faq5a: Yes. The core scanner (packages/core) and all 13 rule patterns are public on GitHub. You can audit exactly what we look for before trusting the tool.
home.faq6q: What's the difference between Free and PRO?
home.faq6a: Free users get 10 scans per month and see the last 5 scans in their dashboard. PRO removes all limits, shows the full history, adds the score trend chart, and enables JSON export.
home.ctaSubtitle: No account required. Sign in only when you want to save your history.
